best-practice-synthesis-routing.reason: This DR clarifies the scope of rule #2 (capability floor vs brand pin) but writes no best_practice_synthesis. No synthesis content is authored; the facts/judgment split is preserved verbatim.

bias_direction: Acute self-serving risk: this is an AI ratifying that a model of its own class is authorized to perform the project's highest-judgment work. The bias runs toward expansively reading the capability floor so the current model qualifies, and toward downplaying that a brand-pin reading would have blocked synthesis until an owner ruling. The mitigation is that the ruling is the owner's, not mine, and the capability-floor logic holds independently of which model is current — it would equally authorize a future non-Anthropic-named top-tier model and equally restrict a future weaker one.
independent_reviewer_counterclaim: An independent reviewer would object that 'capability floor' is unfalsifiable as stated — no benchmark defines 'Opus-class', so the floor collapses to 'whatever model is running'. Counter: the rule's real safeguard was never the model string but the downstream verification gates (#7/#8/#10), which bind every synthesis claim regardless of model and are the dominant cost; the model floor is a coarse backstop, and the reviewer's objection argues for strengthening the gates, not for pinning a superseded brand name that forbids the more capable model.

reattestation.reason: The weak-band delta concerns which cells may be called best practice, not who authors synthesis. This DR's capability-floor rule ('Opus-class or above') is untouched. Verdict unchanged.
